title: Xerocladia viridiramis
taxonomy: Xerocladia viridiramis is a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae. It belongs to the genus Xerocladia, which is a monotypic genus. Xerocladia viridiramis is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is found in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant is classified as a dicotyledonous angiosperm, which means it has two cotyledons in its seedling stage and produces flowers with petals. The classification of Xerocladia viridiramis is still being studied, but it is widely accepted as a member of the Achariaceae family.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
